     The Numbers Business Report

That’s My Boy (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $36,931,089Details
International Box Office $21,948,592
Worldwide Box Office $58,879,681
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,298,505
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,826,833
Total $8,125,338
Further financial details...

Synopsis

While still in his teens, Donny fathered a son, Todd, and raised him as a single parent up until Todd's 18th birthday. Now, after not seeing each other for years, Todd's world comes crashing down on the eve of his wedding when an uninvited Donny suddenly shows up. Trying desperately to reconnect with his son, Donny is now forced to deal with the repercussions of his bad parenting skills.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,453,714 (36.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.75 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:62.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$57,500,000 (worldwide box office is 1.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,030 opening theaters/3,030 max. theaters, 3.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $55,489,425

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 15th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: June 13th, 2012 (Wide), released as That's My Boy (Trinidad)
June 14th, 2012 (Wide), released as That's My Boy (Australia)
June 14th, 2012 (Wide), released as That's My Boy (Curacao)
June 14th, 2012 (Wide), released as That's My Boy (Suriname)
June 14th, 2012 (Wide), released as That's My Boy (Aruba)
Video Release: October 16th, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for crude sexual content throughout, nudity, pervasive language and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2217, 4/4/2012)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Fictionalized Version of Yourself, Gratuitous Cameos
Plot point: Delayed Adulthood, Dysfunctional Family, February / May Romance, IRS, Sex Crimes, Unexpected Families, Unexpected Pregnancy
Social setting: Bachelor Party, Strippers / Stripping, Wedding Day
Subgenre: Satirical Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Relativity Media, Happy Madison
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

While still in his teens, Donny fathered a son, Todd, and raised him as a single parent up until Todd's 18th birthday. Now, after not seeing each other for years, Todd's world comes crashing down on the eve of his wedding when an uninvited Donny suddenly shows up. Trying desperately to reconnect with his son, Donny is now forced to deal with the repercussions of his bad parenting skills.
